title: Efficient Subarray Sum Finder with Incorrect Handling of Negative Integers

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, write a function to find the maximum contiguous subarray sum. Consider a subarray as a contiguous sequence of elements in the array. Note that negative integers are allowed. The function should return the maximum sum of a subarray. If all elements are negative, return the maximum of all elements. If the input array is empty, return 0.
statement.notes: The function should handle edge cases such as empty input array and all negative integers.

constraints.description: The number of elements in the input array

examples.explanation: The maximum subarray sum is [5, -3, 7, -2, 3] which has a sum of 11.
The maximum subarray sum is [-1] which has a sum of -1.
The input array is empty, so the function returns 0.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.betterSolution: Use Kadane's algorithm to find the maximum subarray sum
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement Kadane's algorithm with a time complexity of O(n)

functionSignature.python: def maxSubarraySum(self, arr, n: int) -> int:
